quiz.title: Pre-calculus: Unit 13 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: 13.11: Parametric and Polar Equations (Calculations)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Introduction to parametric, eliminating the parameter, projectile motion, polar coordinate system, polar to rectangular conversion, polar graphs (circles, lines, limacons), polar graphs (roses, lemniscates), symmetry in polar graphs, intersection of polar graphs, and complex numbers in polar form review

questions.question: Given $x = t^2$ and $y = t + 1$, find the coordinates when $t = 2$.
A ball is launched from the ground with initial speed $40$ ft/s at an angle of $30^\circ$. Find the $x$-component of the initial velocity.
Plot the polar point $(5, 120^\circ)$. What are its rectangular coordinates?
Convert the polar equation $r = 4 \cos \theta$ to rectangular form.
Identify the shape of $r = 3 + 2 \cos \theta$.
questions.explanation: $a = 3$, $b = 2$. Since $a > b$ and $a/b = 1.5$ (between 1 and 2), it is a dimpled limacon.

questions.question: Test $r = 2 \sin \theta$ for symmetry about the line $\theta = \pi/2$ (y-axis).
questions.explanation: Replace $\theta$ with $\pi - \theta$: $2 \sin(\pi - \theta) = 2 \sin \theta$. The equation remains the same, so it is symmetric about $\theta = \pi/2$.

questions.question: Find the intersection points of $r = 2$ and $r = 4 \cos \theta$ (other than the pole).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered parametric and polar equations.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Review polar graph shapes and symmetry tests carefully.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing — focus on converting between coordinate systems.
scoreMessages.0: Review the fundamentals of parametric equations and polar coordinates.
